mnemonics: Wires Frame Goals: Use Wires to Frame out your UX Goals. (Wires = Wireframes, Frame = Structure, Goals = UX Objectives)
Blueprint Before Beauty: Wireframes are the blueprint (structure/function) that comes before the visual design (beauty).
Goals Over Graphics: UX Goals (usability, efficiency) are prioritized over graphical details in a wireframe.
keyPoints: A wireframe is a low-fidelity, simplified outline of a digital interface, focusing on structure, layout, and functionality.
The primary UX goals often captured in wireframes include usability, user flow efficiency, content hierarchy, and functionality placement.
Wireframes serve as a communication tool to align stakeholders, gather early feedback, and iterate on the core user experience before visual design.
commonMistakes: Adding high-fidelity visual design elements (like colors, detailed images, final fonts) which distract from the goal of structuring content and functionality.
Focusing on the wireframe as a final product rather than a disposable, iterative tool for discussion and experimentation.
Not aligning the wireframe's structure and layout with previously defined user stories, personas, and core UX objectives.
